purpose: Models the position coder responsible for tool positioning accuracy and spindle orientation control. Tracks signal integrity degradation mechanisms that affect position feedback quality, synchronous error, and orientation completion reliability.

inputs.name: orientation_stop_position
inputs.description: Commanded target orientation stop position for the spindle, ranging 0–4095 pulses.

outputs.name: position_error
outputs.description: Difference between actual and commanded spindle position in pulses; degrades with signal integrity loss.

outputs.name: synchronous_error
outputs.description: Synchronous position error between encoder channels; increases as signal integrity degrades.

outputs.name: orientation_time
outputs.description: Time elapsed to complete spindle orientation; increases as signal degradation worsens.

outputs.name: spindle_sequence_state
outputs.description: Current state in the spindle orientation sequence (a|b|c|d|e); affected by one-rotation signal reliability.

outputs.name: orientation_completed
outputs.description: Boolean flag indicating whether spindle orientation has successfully completed; set to false when signal integrity is critically degraded.

states.name: signal_degradation
states.degradation_timescale: medium-term operational cycles; full degradation expected over ~33 normalized time units at nominal driver
states.description: Health index of the primary position coder signal channel. Starts at 1.0 (healthy) and degrades toward 0.0 as disconnection risk and signal noise accumulate due to mechanical load cycles and spindle speed stress.

states.name: one_rotation_signal_degradation
states.degradation_timescale: medium-term; full degradation expected over ~67 normalized time units at nominal driver
states.description: Health index of the one-rotation reference signal. Degrades due to thermal cycling and mechanical wear affecting the index pulse detection reliability.

states.name: signal_integrity_degradation
states.degradation_timescale: medium-term; full degradation expected over ~100 normalized time units at nominal driver
states.description: Overall signal integrity health index combining effects of contamination, oxidation, and thermal stress on encoder signal quality. Degrades toward 0.0 as signal fidelity is lost.

states.name: contamination_level
states.description: Normalized level of contamination (metallic wear particles, debris) accumulated on or near the encoder. Builds up over time and accelerates signal degradation when elevated.

states.name: position_error_tracking
states.description: Smoothed internal estimate of position error, tracking the instantaneous position error via first-order lag. Used as threshold state for position-error-based events.

events.name: position_coder_disconnected
events.description: Triggered when signal_degradation health index falls below the critical disconnection threshold, indicating the position coder signal has been lost or the physical connection has failed.

events.name: one_rotation_signal_detection_error
events.description: Triggered when one_rotation_signal_degradation falls below the detection error threshold or contamination_level exceeds its warning threshold, indicating intermittent index pulse detection errors.

events.name: one_rotation_signal_not_detected
events.description: Triggered when one_rotation_signal_degradation falls below the critical not-detected threshold, indicating complete loss of the one-rotation reference pulse required for orientation homing.

events.name: position_coder_signal_error
events.description: Triggered when signal_integrity_degradation falls below the signal error threshold or position_error_tracking magnitude exceeds the critical position error threshold, indicating general signal quality degradation causing unreliable position feedback.

parameters.name: signal_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which the primary position coder signal health degrades per unit driver input per unit time. Mapped from bearing_wear_rate as the closest simulator-provided rate for load-cycle-driven signal wear.

parameters.name: one_rotation_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which the one-rotation signal health degrades per unit driver input per unit time. Mapped from preload_degradation_rate as the closest simulator-provided rate for thermal-cycling-and-wear-driven degradation.

parameters.name: signal_integrity_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which overall signal integrity health degrades per unit driver input per unit time. Mapped from grease_degradation_rate as the closest simulator-provided rate for thermal-stress-and-oxidation-driven degradation.

parameters.name: contamination_buildup_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which contamination level accumulates per unit time from metallic wear particles. Taken directly from simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: critical_signal_degradation_threshold
parameters.description: Health index value of signal_degradation below which position_coder_disconnected event is triggered. Mapped from critical_bearing_wear (0.8 wear => 0.2 health remaining => threshold at 1 - 0.8 = 0.2).

parameters.name: one_rotation_detection_error_threshold
parameters.description: Health index value of one_rotation_signal_degradation below which one_rotation_signal_detection_error warning is triggered.

parameters.name: one_rotation_not_detected_threshold
parameters.description: Health index value of one_rotation_signal_degradation below which one_rotation_signal_not_detected failure is triggered.

parameters.name: signal_error_integrity_threshold
parameters.description: Health index value of signal_integrity_degradation below which position_coder_signal_error warning is triggered.

parameters.name: contamination_warning_threshold
parameters.description: Contamination level above which it contributes to triggering one_rotation_signal_detection_error.

parameters.name: critical_position_error
parameters.description: Absolute position error magnitude in pulses above which position_coder_signal_error is triggered. Taken directly from simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: position_error_lag_coefficient
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for position_error_tracking state update. Capped at 0.5 for numerical stability.

parameters.name: base_position_error_scale
parameters.description: Scaling factor converting signal degradation loss into position error magnitude in pulses.

parameters.name: base_synchronous_error_scale
parameters.description: Scaling factor converting signal integrity loss into synchronous error magnitude in pulses.

parameters.name: base_orientation_time
parameters.description: Nominal orientation time in milliseconds under healthy signal conditions.

parameters.name: orientation_time_degradation_scale
parameters.description: Maximum additional orientation time in milliseconds added when signal integrity is fully degraded.

parameters.name: orientation_completion_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum combined signal health required for orientation_completed to be true.

state_transition_logic: signal_degradation: decrease by signal_degradation_rate * load_cycles_and_speed * dt; clamp to [0.0, 1.0].
one_rotation_signal_degradation: decrease by one_rotation_degradation_rate * thermal_cycling_and_wear * dt; clamp to [0.0, 1.0].
signal_integrity_degradation: decrease by signal_integrity_degradation_rate * thermal_stress_and_oxidation * dt; clamp to [0.0, 1.0].
contamination_level: increase by contamination_buildup_rate * metallic_wear_particles * dt; clamp to [0.0, 1.0].

position_error_tracking: approach instantaneous_position_error using first-order lag: position_error_tracking += position_error_lag_coefficient * (instantaneous_position_error - position_error_tracking) * dt, where instantaneous_position_error = base_position_error_scale * (1.0 - signal_degradation); clamp to [-4096.0, 4096.0].
output_logic: position_error: Computed as base_position_error_scale * (1.0 - signal_degradation) * sign_factor, where sign_factor alternates based on orientation_stop_position parity to reflect realistic bidirectional error. As signal_degradation decreases, position_error magnitude increases toward base_position_error_scale.

synchronous_error: Computed as base_synchronous_error_scale * (1.0 - signal_integrity_degradation) * (1.0 + contamination_level). As signal_integrity_degradation decreases or contamination_level increases, synchronous_error magnitude grows toward base_synchronous_error_scale.

orientation_time: Computed as base_orientation_time + orientation_time_degradation_scale * (1.0 - min(signal_degradation, one_rotation_signal_degradation)). As either signal health index decreases, orientation time increases toward base_orientation_time + orientation_time_degradation_scale.

spindle_sequence_state: Determined by the combined health of one_rotation_signal_degradation and signal_integrity_degradation. When both are above 0.5, normal sequence progression (a->b->c->d->e) is reported. When one_rotation_signal_degradation < one_rotation_not_detected_threshold, sequence stalls at state 'b' (awaiting index pulse). When signal_integrity_degradation < signal_error_integrity_threshold, sequence reports state 'c' (error recovery mode).

orientation_completed: Set to true when min(signal_degradation, one_rotation_signal_degradation, signal_integrity_degradation) >= orientation_completion_threshold AND abs(position_error_tracking) < critical_position_error; otherwise false.
event_logic: position_coder_disconnected (SP0027): event_state = (signal_degradation - critical_signal_degradation_threshold) / (1.0 - critical_signal_degradation_threshold); clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Event triggers (event_state <= 0.0) when signal_degradation <= critical_signal_degradation_threshold. At healthy initial state (signal_degradation=1.0), event_state = 1.0.

one_rotation_signal_detection_error (SP0041): event_state = min((one_rotation_signal_degradation - one_rotation_detection_error_threshold) / (1.0 - one_rotation_detection_error_threshold), (contamination_warning_threshold - contamination_level) / contamination_warning_threshold); clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Event triggers when one_rotation_signal_degradation <= one_rotation_detection_error_threshold OR contamination_level >= contamination_warning_threshold. At healthy initial state, event_state = 1.0.

one_rotation_signal_not_detected (SP0042): event_state = (one_rotation_signal_degradation - one_rotation_not_detected_threshold) / (1.0 - one_rotation_not_detected_threshold); clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Event triggers when one_rotation_signal_degradation <= one_rotation_not_detected_threshold. At healthy initial state, event_state = 1.0.

position_coder_signal_error (SP0047): event_state = min((signal_integrity_degradation - signal_error_integrity_threshold) / (1.0 - signal_error_integrity_threshold), (critical_position_error - abs(position_error_tracking)) / critical_position_error); clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Event triggers when signal_integrity_degradation <= signal_error_integrity_threshold OR abs(position_error_tracking) >= critical_position_error. At healthy initial state (signal_integrity_degradation=1.0, position_error_tracking=0.0), event_state = 1.0.
assumptions: signal_degradation is driven by load_cycles_and_speed per simulator ground truth for bearing_wear; this is the closest semantic match for mechanical load-induced encoder signal wear. Rate default 0.02 taken from bearing_wear_rate.

one_rotation_signal_degradation is driven by thermal_cycling_and_wear per simulator ground truth for preload_degradation; thermal cycling is the dominant stressor for index pulse reliability. Rate default 0.015 taken from preload_degradation_rate.

signal_integrity_degradation is driven by thermal_stress_and_oxidation per simulator ground truth for grease_degradation; oxidation and thermal stress are the dominant mechanisms for overall signal quality loss. Rate default 0.03 taken from grease_degradation_rate.
contamination_level is driven by metallic_wear_particles per simulator ground truth. Rate default 0.01 taken from contamination_buildup_rate.
critical_signal_degradation_threshold (0.2) is derived from critical_bearing_wear=0.8, interpreted as 80% wear consumed means 20% health remaining.

one_rotation_detection_error_threshold (0.4) and one_rotation_not_detected_threshold (0.2) are not directly provided in simulator ground truth; derived from degradation_timescale reasoning with warning at 40% health and failure at 20% health. Flagged for calibration.
signal_error_integrity_threshold (0.3) is not directly provided in simulator ground truth; derived as 30% health remaining triggers signal error warning. Flagged for calibration.

position_error_tracking is introduced as a tracking state to allow event thresholding on position error magnitude using only state names, as required by the strict event rules prohibiting direct input references in threshold_states.
The sign_factor in position_error output is a simplification; a full implementation would track actual commanded vs. measured position using orientation_stop_position input.
spindle_sequence_state output is modeled as a discrete enumeration derived from health thresholds; a full implementation may require a separate finite-state-machine sub-model.

overheat_threshold (140.0), speed_arrival_threshold (15.0), zero_speed_threshold (0.75), vibration thresholds, and acceleration thresholds from simulator ground truth are not applicable to the position_coder component and are not used here.
critical_temperature (150.0) and critical_vibration (10.7) from simulator ground truth are not directly observable outputs of this component and are not modeled as states or events here.
